title: Lower bounds for clique vs. independent set
speaker: Mika Göös
abstract: We prove an $\omega(\log n)$ lower bound on the conondeterministic communication complexity of the Clique vs. Independent Set problem introduced by Yannakakis (STOC 1988, JCSS 1991). As a corollary, this implies superpolynomial lower bounds for the Alon--Saks--Seymour conjecture in graph theory. Our approach is to first exhibit a query complexity separation for the decision tree analogue of the UP vs. coNP question---namely, unambiguous DNF width vs. CNF width---and then "lift" this separation over to communication complexity using a result from prior work.

proof_stages.label: Clique-vs-independent-set statement and target separation
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines the communication problem for a fixed graph, recalls its extension-complexity and Alon-Saks-Seymour motivations, and states the main result that the relevant co-nondeterministic complexity can be superlogarithmic, equivalently giving superpolynomial gaps between chromatic number and biclique partition number.

proof_stages.label: Unambiguous communication as disjoint rectangle covers
proof_stages.summary: Nondeterministic protocols are reinterpreted as rectangle covers of a communication matrix, the clique-vs-independent-set protocol is identified as unambiguous, and Yannakakis' construction embeds any efficient unambiguous protocol into a clique-vs-independent-set instance, reducing the theorem to a coNP-versus-UP communication separation.

proof_stages.label: Decision-tree analogues and query-to-communication strategy
proof_stages.summary: The proof strategy is set up by defining co-nondeterministic decision-tree complexity as CNF width and unambiguous decision-tree complexity as unambiguous DNF width, with the plan to prove the separation in query complexity and then lift it to communication complexity.

proof_stages.label: Naive Boolean gap and one-sided composition obstruction
proof_stages.summary: A small Boolean function shows a local gap between unambiguous and co-nondeterministic complexity, but recursive self-composition fails because certifying a top-level one may require certifying hard zero outputs, motivating functions with one hard zero symbol and many easy-to-certify nonzero symbols.

proof_stages.label: Weighted projective-plane pointer functions for a composable query gap
proof_stages.summary: Using pairwise-intersecting hypergraphs from finite projective planes and weighted encodings of pointer values, the construction gives low unambiguous certificates for satisfied edges while forcing many queries to certify the all-zero input, and recursive composition amplifies this base gap.

proof_stages.label: Lifting theorem via composed functions and conical juntas
proof_stages.summary: A composed-function lifting theorem with an inner-product gadget converts communication protocols into conical-junta representations of the outer query function, so nondeterministic communication lower bounds follow from nondeterministic decision-tree lower bounds up to the gadget-size factor.

proof_stages.label: Communication separation and remaining lifting questions
proof_stages.summary: Since communication protocols can trivially simulate the query upper bounds, the query separation lifts to the desired communication and clique-vs-independent-set separation, after which the speaker formulates conjectures about larger gaps and open problems on sharper simulation theorems.

examples.name: Set intersection and set disjointness
examples.note: Set intersection illustrates the basic nondeterministic protocol by guessing an intersecting element, and set disjointness later illustrates the standard composed-function form.
examples.engagement_reason: He describes the nondeterministic certificate protocol for intersection by guessing the intersecting element and relates its log n cost.

examples.name: Three-bit Hamming-weight one-or-two function
examples.note: This tiny Boolean function has unambiguous width 2 and co-nondeterministic width 3, and the failed attempt to amplify it by recursive composition exposes the one-sided obstruction motivating the multi-valued construction.
examples.engagement_reason: He analyzes the specific 3-bit function, giving an unambiguous width-2 DNF argument and proving co-nondeterministic complexity 3 via sensitivity of 000.

examples.name: AND function for conical juntas versus polynomials
examples.note: The AND function illustrates that conical juntas behave more classically and one-sidedly than ordinary approximating polynomials, which can approximate AND at much lower degree.
examples.engagement_reason: He states the contrast that low-degree polynomials approximate AND while conical juntas must read almost all inputs, without proving it on AND.

passes.reason: AND is a permutation-symmetric boundary threshold function with a single accepting input, used as a separating case where conical juntas need high degree while polynomials do not.
